name: Sendero de las Viñas (PRC-BU 71) — circular desde Quemada por Zazuar
region_intro: Hiking around Quemada reveals a landscape characterized by river valleys, such as those of the Duero and Arandilla, and extensive agricultural areas. The terrain often features gentle slopes and open countryside, with routes passing through vineyards and along waterways. This region offers a network of trails suitable for exploring its rural charm and natural features. Best hiking trails around Quemada The most popular hiking route is Sendero de las Viñas (PRC-BU 71) — circular desde Quemada por Zazuar, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of vineyards and rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Duero Path, an easy 2.1 miles (3.4 km) path. This trail follows the Duero River, providing a flat and accessible option. Local hikers also love the Trail of the Valleys, a 11.4 miles (18.4 km) trail leading through varied rural terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Quemada is defined by river paths, agricultural fields, and rolling countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to moderate longer routes. The routes in Quemada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 12 reviews. More than 55 hikers have used komoot to explore Quemada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Quemada?
A: The hiking trails around Quemada are characterized by river valleys, such as those of the Duero and Arandilla, and extensive agricultural areas. You'll find gentle slopes and open countryside, with routes often passing through vineyards and along waterways. The region offers a network of trails suitable for exploring its rural charm and natural features.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Quemada?
A: Yes, Quemada offers several easy hiking options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037989241/duero-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Path&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that follows the Duero River, providing a flat and accessible option perfect for a relaxed stroll.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking around Quemada, you can explore several interesting attractions. Consider visiting the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real of Aranda de Duero&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5507816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranda Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3395685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de la Vid&lt;/a&gt;. The charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Duero&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant point of interest.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Quemada?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131558264/sendero-de-las-vinas-prc-bu-71-circular-desde-quemada-por-zazuar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de las Viñas (PRC-BU 71) — circular desde Quemada por Zazuar&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.7-mile (12.4 km) trail offering views of vineyards and rural landscapes. Another is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040347182/penaranda-de-duero-to-san-juan-del-monte-circular-via-the-rio-arandilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Duero to San Juán del Monte — circular via the Río Arandilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.6-mile (9.1 km) loop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Quemada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 12 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful rural landscapes, the well-maintained paths along the rivers, and the opportunity to experience the region's vineyards and agricultural fields.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Quemada?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes available in Quemada, ranging from easy riverside strolls to more moderate, longer routes through varied rural terrain.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a longer experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131577216/trail-of-the-valleys" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Valleys&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.4-mile (18.4 km) trail leading through varied rural terrain, typically completed in about 5 hours. Another moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040349843/castillo-de-penaranda-de-duero-y-ermita-de-la-virgen-de-la-vega-circular-por-el-rio-arandilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñaranda de Duero y Ermita de la Virgen de la Vega — circular por el Río Arandilla&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.5-mile (16.9 km) route.

Q: Is Quemada a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Quemada offers several options suitable for families. The generally gentle terrain and accessible paths, particularly along the river valleys, make it a pleasant area for walks with children. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037989241/duero-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Path&lt;/a&gt; are ideal for families.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Quemada?
A: The region experiences a varied climate, making it suitable for outdoor enthusiasts throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, offering mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, whether for enjoying spring wildflowers or autumn foliage in the vineyards.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking trailheads near Quemada?
A: Information regarding specific public transport links directly to trailheads is limited. It is generally recommended to use a car to access many of the rural starting points for hikes in the Quemada region, especially for flexibility. However, local bus services might connect larger towns in the area.

Q: Are there parking facilities available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not always listed, many rural hiking areas in Spain typically have informal parking spots or small designated areas near villages or points of interest. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for any user-provided parking information.
tips: The Church of Our Lady of the Assumption in Quemada, Burgos, is a Romanesque church (12th-13th centuries). Its most notable features are the 16th-century Renaissance tower, an 18th-century main altarpiece, and a Renaissance baptismal font.

The Church of Saint Andrew the Apostle in Zazuar was built between the 16th and 19th centuries. Its interior combines Gothic and Renaissance styles and is notable for its altars with 15th-century Castilian panels, a Gothic monstrance, and a processional cross. The masonry and stone tower was completed in 1802.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of Our Lady of Quemada
